task.title: Preserve replay storage error sources
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/354 by retaining SQLite replay failures as structured Rust error sources instead of lossy strings while keeping domain replay failures source-free.
acceptance_criteria: Replay storage failures expose their wrapped rusqlite error through std::error::Error::source.
Unsupported events, malformed payloads, version gaps, and invalid stored versions remain source-free.
Display context, typed replay outcomes, and ReplayError equality remain stable.
Tests exercise an actual replay row-decoding failure and documentation describes the source contract.
The pinned local and required pull-request quality gates pass.

attempts.summary: Added a focused replay source-chain test before changing ReplayError.
attempts.diagnostic: The RED test failed with Rust E0308 because ReplayError::Storage accepted a String rather than the rusqlite::Error needed for a standard error source.
attempts.patch: Constructed storage and domain replay failures and asserted that only the storage variant exposes a rusqlite source.

attempts.summary: Retained the SQLite error through replay mapping and exercised a real row-decoding failure.
attempts.patch: Changed Storage to wrap rusqlite::Error, delegated Error::source for that variant, kept display context unchanged, and corrupted a test payload's SQLite type to drive the production storage-error path.

attempts.summary: Corrected an equality compatibility regression found by independent review.
attempts.diagnostic: The first review found that wrapping rusqlite::Error removed ReplayError's derived Eq and PartialEq traits and forced equality-based callers to stop compiling.
attempts.patch: Restored Eq and PartialEq with the prior display-string equality semantics and restored the existing assert_eq-based compatibility tests.

attempts.summary: Documented and reviewed the unavoidable pre-1.0 storage payload correction.
attempts.diagnostic: A follow-up review noted that changing Storage from String to rusqlite::Error is source-breaking for direct constructors and pattern matches; preserving the String payload cannot also provide a borrowed structured source.
attempts.patch: Kept the smallest source-preserving representation, documented the intentional pre-1.0 correction in the pull request, and preserved the variant name, display context, and equality traits. CodeRabbit passed with one nonblocking suggestion to prefer structural PartialEq.

outcome.summary: Typed replay now preserves SQLite causes for conventional Rust error chains, domain replay failures remain source-free, and equality compatibility survives the representation correction.

verification.command: GitHub Rust quality gate for pull request 355

lessons: Converting infrastructure errors to strings at an API boundary permanently discards the standard Rust error chain.
A focused source test should drive the real mapping path, not only construct the public error variant directly.
Changing a wrapped error type can silently remove derived compatibility traits; independent review should check trait-level API regressions as well as runtime behavior.
Before 1.0, an unavoidable representation correction should be explicit, narrowly scoped, and preserve variant names, display context, and useful comparison behavior where possible.
